name: debug-and-fix-all-video-20260325-0525
idea: Debug and fix all video editor bugs: (1) Player keeps re-rendering/stopping-starting - investigate if React 18 + Remotion causes constant re-renders, or if debounced save/React Query invalidation causes it; (2) Remove timeline list page - back button goes to home, auto-default into main timeline; (3) Fix render button (currently stub); (4) Fix drag-from-GenerationsPane into assets/timeline; (5) Fix text editing appearing small during edit; (6) Fix play button blocked by overlay/asset z-index; (7) General stability audit of the video editor

history.message: Codex step failed with exit code 1

overrides.note:
CRITICAL FORMAT: Use FLAT ## Step N: headings at h2 level. NO ## Phase headers.

USER BUG REPORTS:

1. PLAYER RE-RENDERING: The Remotion Player keeps stopping and starting. Likely causes:
   - useTimelineData has a debounced save that fires every 500ms - this could trigger React Query cache updates
   - The save changes the config signature, which triggers a re-render of the Player
   - React 18 strict mode double-renders could interact badly with Remotion
   - The resolvedConfig object might be recreated on every render (not memoized), causing Player to remount
   - Check if useTimelineData polls (refetchInterval) and creates new config objects each time
   - Key fix: memoize the resolvedConfig, stabilize the Player component's props

2. REMOVE TIMELINE LIST PAGE: 
   - Currently VideoEditorPage shows TimelineList when no ?timeline= param
   - Instead: auto-load the most recent timeline (or create one) and go straight to the editor
   - Back button in the editor should navigate to / (home) not /tools/video-editor
   - The timeline list is unnecessary UX friction

3. RENDER BUTTON STUB:
   - startRender() in useTimelineData.ts is a stub
   - For now, just remove or disable the render button since there's no backend renderer
   - Or show a toast saying "Render not available yet"

4. DRAG FROM GENERATIONS PANE:
   - The polish plan was supposed to add this but it may not be working
   - Check if the drop handlers on TimelineEditor and AssetPanel are set up
   - Check if MediaGalleryItem's drag data includes the right format
   - The drop should add the asset to the registry and create a clip

5. TEXT EDITING SMALL:
   - OverlayEditor double-click creates an inline text edit
   - The text appears small - likely the overlay coordinate mapping is wrong
   - The overlay maps between canvas space and composition space
   - Check the scale factor calculation in OverlayEditor.tsx

6. PLAY BUTTON BLOCKED:
   - The play button in PreviewPanel is behind another element
   - This is a z-index issue - the OverlayEditor or some other element is covering the controls
   - The play/pause controls need pointer-events or higher z-index

7. GENERAL STABILITY:
   - Check for unnecessary re-renders in the editor component tree
   - React.memo the heavy components (RemotionPreview, TimelineEditor)
   - Stabilize context values with useMemo
   - Check if the 3 contexts are causing cascading re-renders

USER NOTE: We could upgrade to React 19 (what the original announcement-video repo uses) if it would help with stability. Consider this as an option for fixing the re-render issues. Also: reduce poll interval to much longer (or disable polling entirely and rely on realtime), keep render infrastructure as stubs for now, drag from GenerationsPane was partially implemented in the polish plan but may not be fully wired.

REVISION GUIDANCE:

1. resolvedConfig-memo-wrong: Use a separate configSignatureRef that tracks the last signature. Only update the memoized resolvedConfig when the signature actually changes. Pattern: useRef for last signature, compare in useEffect, only set state when different.

2. remotion-metadata-remount: The metadata (fps, durationInFrames, compositionWidth, compositionHeight) must also be memoized with the same signature-based stability. Either memo metadata separately or derive it from the stable resolvedConfig. Key insight: even if inputProps is stable, changing durationInFrames/fps props on <Player> will cause it to remount.

3. step8-scope-creep: Remove the aggressive refactoring. Keep stability audit to just React.memo on heavy components and stabilizing context values. Don't throttle playback context or refactor to ref+subscription pattern.

4. step3-race-condition: Show a loading spinner while timelines are loading, then auto-navigate. Use a brief loading state rather than a flash of empty content.

1. resolvedConfig-memo-wrong: Use a separate configSignatureRef that tracks the last signature. Only update the memoized resolvedConfig when the signature actually changes. Pattern: useRef for last signature, compare in useEffect, only set state when different.

last_gate.rationale: All 5 flags from iteration 1 are resolved. Weighted score dropped from 4.0 to 0. The plan delta of 61% reflects substantive revisions that addressed the two significant correctness flags (ref-based signature pattern, metadata memoization) and scoped down Step 8. No recurring critiques, no scope creep, no open flags. Preflight is clean. The plan is ready for execution.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Weighted score trajectory 4.0 → 0 across 2 iterations. Plan delta 61.4% was warranted — it incorporated user revision guidance for 4 named issues. All 5 flags resolved (2 significant correctness, 1 scope creep, 1 race condition, 1 data shape verification). Zero recurring critiques. Preflight checks all pass. The plan is converged and executable.
warnings: Step 6 text-edit fix: verify the actual data shape of meta[overlay.actionId]?.text?.fontSize at runtime — the flag was resolved as 'acknowledged' but the executor should confirm the path exists before coding the fix.
Step 5 drag-drop: this is the most investigative step — if MediaGalleryItem doesn't wire setGenerationDragData, the fix scope grows. Budget extra time here.

settled_decisions.decision: Use configSignatureRef + stableResolvedConfigRef pattern (not useMemo with data?.signature dep) to stabilize resolvedConfig identity.
settled_decisions.rationale: Both iterations converged on this as the correct approach. The ref-based pattern is robust against data object replacement with identical content.

settled_decisions.decision: RemotionPreview metadata useMemo deps should be primitive values (fps, resolution string, clips) not the config object.
settled_decisions.rationale: Addresses the remotion-metadata-remount flag — even stable inputProps won't prevent remount if durationInFrames/fps props change identity.

settled_decisions.decision: Reduce refetchInterval from 2s to 30s on both timeline and asset registry queries.
settled_decisions.rationale: User approved. Realtime handles multi-tab sync. Reduces poll-triggered object churn.

settled_decisions.decision: Render button shows toast, keeps infrastructure as stubs. No error state changes.
settled_decisions.rationale: User confirmed: keep render infrastructure for future use.

settled_decisions.decision: Step 8 limited to React.memo verification and context dep audit. No throttling, no ref+subscription refactoring.
settled_decisions.rationale: Gate-approved scope reduction to prevent scope creep.

settled_decisions.decision: Show loading spinner during timeline fetch, use replace:true on setSearchParams, guard creation with ref.
settled_decisions.rationale: Prevents flash of empty content and browser history pollution.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize.
